edges.description: Loss of pterin enzyme activity depletes the BH4 cofactor pool.
edges.source: BH4 Biosynthesis and Regeneration Gene Defect

edges.description: Cofactor loss disables phenylalanine, tyrosine, and tryptophan hydroxylases.
edges.target: Impaired Aromatic Amino Acid Hydroxylase Activity

edges.description: Loss of phenylalanine hydroxylase activity raises blood phenylalanine.
edges.source: Impaired Aromatic Amino Acid Hydroxylase Activity

edges.description: Loss of tyrosine and tryptophan hydroxylase activity reduces dopamine, norepinephrine, and serotonin synthesis.
edges.source: Impaired Aromatic Amino Acid Hydroxylase Activity

edges.description: Untreated hyperphenylalaninemia contributes to neurotoxicity and neurodevelopmental impairment alongside the monoamine deficit.
intermediate_mechanisms: brain phenylalanine accumulation and large-neutral-amino-acid transport competition

edges.description: Dopamine, norepinephrine, and serotonin deficiency produce dystonia, parkinsonism, developmental delay, hypotonia, and seizures.
Loss of dopaminergic tone disinhibits pituitary prolactin secretion, producing hyperprolactinemia.
intermediate_mechanisms: reduced hypothalamic dopaminergic inhibition of lactotrophs

edges.description: BH4-deficiency neurologic disease commonly manifests as global developmental delay.
Axial/generalized hypotonia is a frequent presentation.
Severe BH4 deficiency (especially PTPSD/DHPRD) can include epilepsy.
Elevated blood phenylalanine reports impaired BH4-dependent PAH activity.
Low CSF HVA and 5-HIAA report reduced central dopamine and serotonin synthesis.

edges.target: BH4 Biosynthesis and Regeneration Gene Defect
BH4 Biosynthesis and Regeneration Gene Defect

BH4 Biosynthesis and Regeneration Gene Defect
BH4 Biosynthesis and Regeneration Gene Defect

nodes.description: Serotonin precursor that bypasses BH4-dependent tryptophan hydroxylation to restore central serotonin synthesis; combined with levodopa.

evidence.reference_title: Tetrahydrobiopterin deficiencies: Lesson from clinical experience.
evidence.snippet: All patients received treatment with l-dopa and 5-hydroxytryptophan

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic variants in BH4 de novo synthesis genes (GCH1, PTS, SPR) or regeneration genes (QDPR, PCBD1) initiate disease by abolishing or reducing the corresponding pterin enzyme activity.
nodes.id: BH4 Biosynthesis and Regeneration Gene Defect

evidence.reference_title: Consensus guideline for the diagnosis and treatment of tetrahydrobiopterin (BH4) deficiencies.
evidence.snippet: characterized by insufficient synthesis of the monoamine neurotransmitters dopamine and serotonin due to a disturbance of BH4 biosynthesis or recycling.

evidence.reference_title: Mouse models for inherited monoamine neurotransmitter disorders.
evidence.snippet: tetrahydrobiopterin (BH4) cofactor synthesis and recycling (adGTPCH1/DRD, arGTPCH1, PTPS, SR, DHPR)

genetic_context.description: Biallelic pathogenic variants in a BH4 synthesis or regeneration gene.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Features of Families with a Novel Pathogenic Mutation in Sepiapterin Reductase.
evidence.snippet: Elevated cerebrospinal fluid sepiapterin and biopterin levels, along with low neurotransmitter levels, were concordant with a genetic diagnosis of SRD

readouts.interpretation: Low CSF HVA and 5-HIAA report reduced central dopamine and serotonin synthesis.

evidence.reference_title: Sepiapterin Reductase Deficiency Misdiagnosed as Neurological Sequelae of Meningitis.
evidence.snippet: The clinical manifestations include motor and speech delay, axial hypotonia, dystonia, weakness, oculogyric crises, diurnal fluctuation, and improvement of symptoms during sleep.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Recessive Guanosine Triphosphate Cyclohydrolase I Deficiency: Redefining the Phenotypic Spectrum and Outcomes.
evidence.snippet: The GCH1 gene encodes the enzyme guanosine triphosphate cyclohydrolase I (GTPCH), which catalyzes the rate-limiting step in the biosynthesis of tetrahydrobiopterin (BH4), a critical cofactor in the production of monoamine neurotransmitters.

evidence.reference_title: PTS-Related Tetrahydrobiopterin Deficiency (PTPSD).
evidence.snippet: clinical symptoms may become apparent in the neonatal period and can include hypotonia, movement disorders, abnormal eye movements, autonomic dysregulation, and impaired development.

evidence.reference_title: Tetrahydrobiopterin deficiencies: Lesson from clinical experience.
evidence.snippet: the most common clinical symptoms are developmental delay, intellectual disability, and movement disorders.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Features of Families with a Novel Pathogenic Mutation in Sepiapterin Reductase.
evidence.snippet: variants in the SPR gene, which may lead to developmental delays, psychomotor retardation, and cognitive impairments

evidence.reference_title: Consensus guideline for the diagnosis and treatment of tetrahydrobiopterin (BH4) deficiencies.
evidence.snippet: Hyperphenylalaninemia (HPA) is the first diagnostic hallmark for most BH4 deficiencies, apart from autosomal dominant guanosine triphosphate cyclohydrolase I deficiency and sepiapterin reductase deficiency.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated blood phenylalanine reports impaired BH4-dependent PAH activity.

evidence.reference_title: PTS-Related Tetrahydrobiopterin Deficiency (PTPSD).
evidence.snippet: Other features of the condition can include psychiatric comorbidities (ADHD, anxiety, depression), infant feeding difficulties leading to early growth failure, hyperprolactinemia

nodes.description: BH4-dependent phenylalanine hydroxylase, tyrosine hydroxylase, and tryptophan hydroxylase lose activity, simultaneously blocking phenylalanine clearance and catecholamine/serotonin precursor synthesis.
nodes.id: Impaired Aromatic Amino Acid Hydroxylase Activity

evidence.reference_title: PTS-Related Tetrahydrobiopterin Deficiency (PTPSD).
evidence.snippet: an important cofactor for phenylalanine hydroxylase (PAH), tyrosine hydroxylase, and tryptophan hydroxylase.

nodes.description: Dopamine precursor (with a peripheral decarboxylase inhibitor) replaces deficient central dopamine; core neurotransmitter therapy across AR-GTPCHD, PTPSD, SRD, and DHPRD.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Features of Families with a Novel Pathogenic Mutation in Sepiapterin Reductase.
evidence.snippet: SRD patients manifest a broad constellation of symptoms, albeit well-managed using low-dose L-dopa/carbidopa.

nodes.description: Reduced dopamine, norepinephrine, and serotonin synthesis from BH4-dependent tyrosine and tryptophan hydroxylase failure; the clinically dominant mechanism, reflected by low CSF homovanillic acid and 5-hydroxyindoleacetic acid.

evidence.reference_title: Consensus guideline for the diagnosis and treatment of tetrahydrobiopterin (BH4) deficiencies.
evidence.snippet: Early supplementation of neurotransmitter precursors and where appropriate, treatment of HPA results in significant improvement of motor and cognitive function.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Features of Families with a Novel Pathogenic Mutation in Sepiapterin Reductase.
evidence.snippet: Elevated cerebrospinal fluid sepiapterin and biopterin levels, along with low neurotransmitter levels, were concordant with a genetic diagnosis of SRD

nodes.description: Convergent neurological phenotype of BH4 deficiency: developmental delay, hypotonia, dystonia, parkinsonism, oculogyric crises, and seizures, often unresponsive to phenylalanine restriction alone and requiring neurotransmitter precursor therapy.

evidence.reference_title: PTS-Related Tetrahydrobiopterin Deficiency (PTPSD).
evidence.snippet: Neurologic symptoms (dysarthria, dystonia, tremors, abnormal gait, parkinsonism, oculogyric crises, motor tics) may be ameliorated by treatment with sapropterin dihydrochloride and neurotransmitter precursors.

evidence.reference_title: Sepiapterin Reductase Deficiency Misdiagnosed as Neurological Sequelae of Meningitis.
evidence.snippet: The clinical manifestations include motor and speech delay, axial hypotonia, dystonia, weakness, oculogyric crises, diurnal fluctuation, and improvement of symptoms during sleep.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in PCBD1 cause hypomagnesemia and renal magnesium wasting.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in PCBD1 are causative for transient neonatal hyperphenylalaninemia and primapterinuria (HPABH4D).

evidence.reference_title: PTS-Related Tetrahydrobiopterin Deficiency (PTPSD).
evidence.snippet: The molecular diagnosis of PTPSD is established in a proband by identification of biallelic pathogenic (or likely pathogenic) variants in PTS by molecular genetic testing.

evidence.reference_title: PTS-Related Tetrahydrobiopterin Deficiency (PTPSD).
evidence.snippet: Neurologic symptoms (dysarthria, dystonia, tremors, abnormal gait, parkinsonism, oculogyric crises, motor tics) may be ameliorated by treatment with sapropterin dihydrochloride and neurotransmitter precursors.

nodes.description: Dietary phenylalanine restriction used when sapropterin is unavailable, to reduce hyperphenylalaninemia in HPA-associated BH4 deficiencies; insufficient alone for the neurotransmitter deficit.

evidence.reference_title: PTS-Related Tetrahydrobiopterin Deficiency (PTPSD).
evidence.snippet: If sapropterin is not available, dietary Phe restriction should be implemented.

evidence.reference_title: Genome sequencing identifies a homozygous inversion disrupting QDPR as a cause for dihydropteridine reductase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic pathogenic variants in QDPR gene lead to BH4‐deficient HPA, accompanied with a severe biogenic amines deficiency

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Features of Families with a Novel Pathogenic Mutation in Sepiapterin Reductase.
evidence.snippet: Sepiapterin Reductase Deficiency (SRD) is a rare inherited neurometabolic disorder caused by variants in the SPR gene

nodes.description: Tetrahydrobiopterin cofactor replacement to lower blood phenylalanine in HPA-associated BH4 deficiencies (PTPSD, AR-GTPCHD, some DHPRD); limited CNS access means it does not normalize central monoamine synthesis alone.

evidence.reference_title: PTS-Related Tetrahydrobiopterin Deficiency (PTPSD).
evidence.snippet: Immediate therapy with sapropterin (tetrahydrobiopterin dihydrochloride; BH4), a cofactor/cosubstrate of PAH, is recommended to reduce blood Phe concentrations in individuals with hyperphenylalaninemia.

evidence.reference_title: Neonatal screening and genotype-phenotype correlation of hyperphenylalaninemia in the Chinese population.
evidence.snippet: Serious clinical manifestations of HPA include irreversible brain damage, intellectual deficiency, and epilepsy

nodes.description: Depletion of the BH4 cofactor that is obligatory for the aromatic amino acid hydroxylases phenylalanine, tyrosine, and tryptophan hydroxylase.

evidence.reference_title: PTS-Related Tetrahydrobiopterin Deficiency (PTPSD).
evidence.snippet: results in a lack of tetrahydropterin, an important cofactor for phenylalanine hydroxylase (PAH), tyrosine hydroxylase, and tryptophan hydroxylase.
